context: By 1970, the Group Sounds boom had faded and Japan's music scene diversified into enka, folk, and early New Music. The year's #1 hit 'Kuro Neko no Tango' was a children's song adapted from an Italian original, reflecting Japan's cultural exchange with Europe. Comedy group The Drifters scored big, while enka singer Fujii Keiko placed two songs in the top five.

tracks.title: Kuro Neko no Tango (Black Cat's Tango)
Keiko no Yume wa Yoru Hiraku
Ai wa Kizutsuki Yasuku (Love is Easily Wounded)
